step_by_step_thinking: Anchorage refers to a method used in orthodontics to stabilize parts of the jaw or teeth during procedures involving movement. From the articles reviewed, stationary anchorage means keeping certain areas of the jaw immobile while others undergo change. None mention reciprocal anchorage specifically, meaning B - Stationary would be the closest match since it implies holding something still relative to moving another part. Therefore,
raw_response:
Anchorage refers to a method used in orthodontics to stabilize parts of the jaw or teeth during procedures involving movement. From the articles reviewed, stationary anchorage means keeping certain areas of the jaw immobile while others undergo change. None mention reciprocal anchorage specifically, meaning B - Stationary would be the closest match since it implies holding something still relative to moving another part. Therefore,
\boxed{B}
